{{Quote|This is Morgan Katarn, leader of the Rebels on Sulon, the only moon of Sullust. I don't have much time left, but with luck, this protocol droid will escape the battle and spread the images of this bloodbath to every planet in the galaxy. To make sure everyone knows our story, I'm recording exactly what happened.|Morgan Katarn recording the events of the subjugation into [[A-Cee]]|audio=SubjugationOfSulon-DFSftE.flac|<ref name="Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire audio drama">[[Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire'' audio drama]]</ref>}}
In [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]], during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Imperial Military/Legends|forces]] under the command of [[High Inquisitor]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] '''subjugated''' the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]].

==Background==
Prior to [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]], Sulon colonists who were opposed to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub Corporation's]] handling of the moon, quietly organized themselves in armed [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|resistance]]. They constructed a geothermal energy distribution system called [[Geothermal tap|G-Tap]] to secure their independence from outside power sources and organized colonization efforts to the planet [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]].

Eager to clamp down on resistance activity, the Empire sent a small [[Imperial Military/Legends|force]] supervised by the [[High Inquisitor]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] to subjugate Sulon and eradicate the Rebel presence there. Only an [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]] was sent, as it was believed the operation would not require any larger vessel. The [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]] Jerec's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], [[Vengeance (Imperial-class, Jerec)|''Vengeance'']], was commanded by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] - assigned by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] himself<ref name="TNEGtC">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> - while the ground forces were led by [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Noda]].

==The battle==
The military operation was conducted with a dual purpose in mind, both destroying the Rebels on Sulon and at the same time lay the blame on the Rebel Alliance by making it out to be their doing. To this end, all soldiers and vehicles were marked with Rebel insignias, although the hoax was only partially successful.

Special elite soldiers from the Empire's [[Ghost Battalion]] attempted to track down the local Rebel leader, [[Morgan Katarn]]. They destroyed eleven homesteads including [[Katarn estate|Katarn's]] and [[Danga estate|Danga's]] and massacred entire families in the process, all in an effort to discredit the Rebels further. Although the raid was successful, they failed to locate Katarn.

After surviving the initial assault, Katarn reunited with other Rebels who decided to get organized and resist further, thinking that the primary target was their [[Geothermal tap|G-Tap]] installation in order to force them purchase a [[fusion plant]] and pay taxes. Katarn believed that they should keep hiding, because the attack was only a ruse to expose and destroy them; [[Skorg Jameson]]'s voice overruled Katarn's saying that the Sulonese should protect their autonomy and prove their power in the Rebellion.

The Rebels voted for war and organized at the installation as highly modified Imperial [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droids]] espied on the participants, and reported their movements to Jerec.

[[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-ATs]] and [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-STs]] made their way towards the G-Tap, supported by modified assault craft. The colonists lost two [[Squad/Legends|squads]] in the first thirty seconds. Katarn also lost most of his team.

After hours of fighting, the remaining Rebels barricaded themselves in the cavern, and attempted to hold out for as long as possible. Despite their efforts, the two contingents of stormtroopers pushed them in the main chamber and overwhelmed them after a brief fight. Most of the Rebels were killed, save the leaders who were brought on-board ''Vengeance'' to be interrogated. Katarn, who was Jerec's primary target, was finally brought before him; refusing to cooperate, the old man was executed by the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] himself.<ref name="Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire2">''[[Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire]]''</ref>

==Aftermath==
[[File:Kyle_Morgan_death.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Kyle with a recording showing his father's fate.]]

Having removed all resistance from the moon, the Galactic Empire took direct control and the Inquisitor Jerec became [[Governor/Legends|Governor]], having a [[Government House (Sulon)|Government House]] built in the center of the capital, [[Barons Hed]].

Outside the city walls, the heads of Rebels were put on display, with Morgan Katarn's head being prominently displayed, impaled upon a spear made up of bones by one of Jerec's [[Seven Dark Jedi|Dark Jedi minions]], [[Maw (Dark Jedi)|Maw]].<ref name="Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II">''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]''</ref>

[[Kyle Katarn]] received word of his father's death at the hands of Rebels, and became more dedicated to his role in the Empire. He would eventually learn the truth of the slaughter and defect to the Rebels, and eventually became a hero of the Alliance, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], and the [[New Jedi Order]], in addition to avenging Morgan's death.
